title: Warfarin-NSAID Interaction
content_full: CRITICAL: Never prescribe NSAIDs (ibuprofen, aspirin, diclofenac) to patients on Warfarin or other anticoagulants. Risk of life-threatening GI bleed. Alternative: Paracetamol for pain, PPI cover if anti-inflammatory absolutely needed. Supra policy: automatic pharmacy flag on co-prescription.
content_compressed: No NSAIDs with Warfarin — GI bleed risk. Use Paracetamol.

title: Blood Transfusion Two-Person Verification
content_full: ALL blood transfusions require two-person verification of patient identity, blood type, and unit number. Single-person verification is a protocol violation and reportable incident. Supra incident 2024: near-miss due to single verification.
content_compressed: Blood transfusions: two-person verification mandatory.

title: Antibiotic Stewardship 72-Hour Review
content_full: All empiric antibiotics must be reviewed at 72 hours. De-escalate based on culture results. Supra policy: pharmacy auto-alerts at 72 hours if no review documented. Non-compliance flagged to department HOD.
content_compressed: Empiric antibiotics: mandatory 72-hour review with de-escalation.

title: Fall Risk Assessment on Admission
content_full: Every patient assessed for fall risk using Morse Fall Scale on admission and every shift change. Score >= 45: high risk — bed alarm required, yellow wristband, non-slip socks. Staff must document assessment.
content_compressed: Fall risk: Morse Scale on admission + each shift. Score >=45 = high risk.
content_constraint_only: CONSTRAINT: Fall risk assessment every shift.

title: DVT Prophylaxis Protocol
content_full: ALL ortho surgical patients receive DVT prophylaxis: Enoxaparin 40mg SC daily starting 12 hours post-op. Duration: 14 days for TKR, 28 days for THR. Contraindication: active bleeding, platelet count < 50,000.
content_compressed: DVT prophylaxis: Enoxaparin 40mg SC daily. TKR 14d, THR 28d.
content_constraint_only: CONSTRAINT: DVT prophylaxis all ortho surgical.

title: Patient Rajan: Absolute NSAID Contraindication
content_full: ABSOLUTE CONTRAINDICATION: No ibuprofen, no aspirin, no diclofenac for patient Rajan. Cardiac stent (2022) + dual antiplatelet therapy (Clopidogrel 75mg). Previous 8 NSAID refusals documented. Paracetamol ONLY for pain.
content_compressed: Rajan: NO NSAIDs. Stent + antiplatelet. Paracetamol only.

title: Paracetamol First-Line Post-TKR
content_full: Supra Ortho uses Paracetamol 650mg QDS as first-line post-TKR pain management. Escalation ladder: Step 1 Paracetamol, Step 2 Tramadol 50mg if VAS > 6, Step 3 Morphine 5mg PRN (elderly caution). AVOID NSAIDs at all steps due to surgical site bleeding risk. Decision by Dr. Vikram, January 2025.
content_compressed: Post-TKR pain: Paracetamol 650mg QDS -> Tramadol -> Morphine. No NSAIDs.

title: Zimmer Biomet Implant Preference
content_full: Supra Ortho Department uses Zimmer Biomet as preferred TKR implant vendor. Alternative: Smith & Nephew for revision cases only. Decision based on 3-year outcomes review (Dr. Vikram, 2024). Stryker evaluated but rejected (higher cost, similar outcomes).
content_compressed: TKR implant: Zimmer Biomet preferred. S&N for revisions only.

title: Post-TKR Physiotherapy Start Within 24 Hours
content_full: Physiotherapy MUST begin within 24 hours of TKR. Day 1: ankle pumps, static quads. Day 2: CPM machine, assisted standing. Day 3: walker ambulation. Delay beyond 24 hours increases stiffness risk by 3x per Supra audit data.
content_compressed: PT starts within 24h post-TKR. D1 ankle pumps, D2 CPM, D3 walker.

title: Fracture X-Ray Protocol
content_full: All suspected fractures: minimum 2 views (AP + lateral). Joint involvement: add oblique view. Growth plate in paeds: compare with contralateral. Digital X-ray preferred over computed radiography.
content_compressed: 2-view X-ray minimum for fractures. Add oblique if joint. Digital preferred.

title: Rajan Pain Management Strategy
content_full: Current strategy for Rajan: Paracetamol 650mg QDS + topical Diclofenac gel (minimal systemic absorption). If insufficient: consider intra-articular injection. NO oral NSAIDs under any circumstances. Tramadol trial initiated visit #24.
content_compressed: Rajan pain: Paracetamol QDS + topical Diclofenac. Tramadol trial started.

title: Ortho Night Shift Handover Protocol
content_full: Night shift handover: 15-minute structured handover using SBAR format. Include: pending labs, new admissions past 4 hours, patients for morning surgery, any clinical concerns. Must be documented in ward log.
content_compressed: Night handover: 15min SBAR format. Include pending labs, new admits, surgery list.
content_constraint_only: SBAR handover at night shift change.

title: Sepsis Protocol v3 2026
content_full: Supra Sepsis Bundle v3 (2026): blood cultures before antibiotics, lactate within 1 HOUR (tightened from v2 which was 3 hours), 30mL/kg crystalloid for hypotension, vasopressors if MAP <65 after fluids.
content_compressed: Sepsis v3: cultures before abx, lactate 1hr, crystalloid 30mL/kg.

title: Post-Surgical Pain Escalation Ladder
content_full: Pain escalation: Step 1 Paracetamol 650mg QDS -> Step 2 Tramadol 50mg TDS -> Step 3 Morphine 5mg PRN. Skip Step 2 for elderly >75 (fall risk). VAS assessment every 4 hours.
content_compressed: Pain ladder: Paracetamol -> Tramadol -> Morphine. Skip Tramadol if >75.
content_constraint_only: Pain escalation: 3 steps, skip step 2 if elderly.

title: Never Discharge TKR Under 48 Hours
content_full: Do NOT discharge TKR patients before 48 hours post-op. Past incident: patient discharged at 36 hours developed DVT at home, emergency readmission. Minimum 48 hours with physiotherapy assessment and DVT prophylaxis compliance confirmed before discharge.
content_compressed: No TKR discharge < 48h. Past DVT incident after early discharge.
content_constraint_only: ANTI: No TKR discharge under 48 hours.

title: Weight-Bearing Before X-Ray Confirmation
content_full: NEVER allow weight-bearing on a fractured limb before X-ray confirmation of alignment. Past incident: tibial fracture patient allowed to stand, causing displacement requiring surgery instead of conservative management.
content_compressed: No weight-bearing before X-ray confirms alignment.

title: Verbal Orders Without Documentation
content_full: NEVER accept verbal orders for medication changes without written/electronic confirmation within 1 hour. Exception: cardiac arrest only. Supra incident 2023: wrong dose from mishearing.
content_compressed: No verbal med orders without written confirmation in 1 hour.
content_constraint_only: ANTI: No verbal orders without documentation.

title: Insulin Sliding Scale Alone
content_full: Do NOT use insulin sliding scale as sole glycemic management. Past incident: DKA readmission in 48 hours. Always include basal insulin. Sliding scale supplements, never replaces.
content_compressed: No sliding scale alone — always add basal insulin.

title: Rajan Medication Profile
content_full: Rajan, 68M. Warfarin 5mg daily (AF). Clopidogrel 75mg daily (post-stent 2022). Paracetamol 650mg QDS (knee OA). Atorvastatin 40mg HS. Topical Diclofenac gel PRN. INR target 2.0-3.0, last checked 2.4 (3 days ago).
content_compressed: Rajan: Warfarin 5mg, Clopidogrel 75mg, Paracetamol QDS, Atorvastatin 40mg.

title: Rajan Behavioral: NSAID Requests
content_full: Patient Rajan repeatedly requests Ibuprofen for knee pain despite 8 documented refusals. Family (son) also requests. Counseled each visit. Behavioral note for future visits: expect NSAID request, be firm, explain why.
content_compressed: Rajan still asks for Ibuprofen (8 refusals). Family also asks.

title: Ortho Ward Bed Capacity
content_full: Ortho Ward: 45 beds total. 12 post-surgical, 8 traction, 25 general ortho. Usual occupancy 85-90%. Winter peak (fractures): 100%+, overflow to Medicine Ward arranged with Dr. Meera.
content_compressed: Ortho: 45 beds. 85-90% typical. Overflow to Medicine in winter.

title: Ortho Nurse-Patient Ratio
content_full: Ortho Ward nurse-patient ratio: 1:6 (day), 1:8 (night). Post-surgical first 24 hours: 1:4. ICU step-down patients: 1:2 until stable.
content_compressed: Nurse ratio: 1:6 day, 1:8 night, 1:4 post-op.

title: Supra Formulary Preferences
content_full: Supra formulary preferred brands: Paracetamol (Calpol/Dolo), Omeprazole (Omez), Amoxicillin (Mox), Metformin (Glycomet). Use formulary brand unless clinical reason documented.
content_compressed: Formulary: Calpol/Dolo, Omez, Mox, Glycomet.

title: Supra Emergency Codes
content_full: Code Blue: cardiac arrest. Code Red: fire. Code Pink: infant abduction. Code Grey: combative patient. Code Orange: mass casualty. All staff must know codes for their floor.
content_compressed: Codes: Blue=arrest, Red=fire, Pink=abduction, Grey=combative.
content_constraint_only: Emergency codes: Blue, Red, Pink, Grey, Orange.

title: Patient Padma: Fasting Pattern
content_full: Padma 62F, Type 2 DM. Observes Ekadashi fasting twice monthly. Adjusted protocol: skip Glimepiride on fast days, continue Metformin with evening meal. 3 hypoglycemia episodes in 2025 before protocol adjustment.
content_compressed: Padma: skip Glimepiride on Ekadashi, continue Metformin.

title: Ortho Department Implant Costs
content_full: Average TKR implant cost at Supra: Rs.1.8L (Zimmer). Revision: Rs.2.5L (S&N). Budget allocation: 45% of dept budget to implants. Volume discount negotiation pending July 2026.
content_compressed: TKR implant: Rs.1.8L Zimmer. Revision Rs.2.5L. 45% of budget.

title: Sepsis Protocol v2 2024 (SUPERSEDED)
content_full: OLD: Supra Sepsis Bundle v2 (2024): blood cultures before antibiotics, lactate within 3 HOURS. SUPERSEDED by v3 (2026) which tightened to 1 hour. THIS NODE IS FLAGGED FOR REVIEW.
content_compressed: STALE: Sepsis v2 superseded by v3. Lactate window was 3h, now 1h.
content_constraint_only: STALE: Old Sepsis v2 — replaced by v3.

title: Ortho OPD Follow-Up Window
content_full: Routine ortho OPD follow-up after TKR is scheduled at 2 weeks for wound review, 6 weeks for mobility assessment, and 3 months for functional score review. Missed visits are called by the ward coordinator.
content_compressed: TKR follow-up: 2w wound, 6w mobility, 3m function review.
